steps.title: Distribute the monomial
steps.content:
Multiply $x^{-7}y^5z^4$ by each term in the second factor.

$$(x^{-7}y^5z^4)(x^3z^6)+(x^{-7}y^5z^4)(y^7z^{-9})$$

steps.title: Add matching exponents
steps.content:
When multiplying powers with the same base, add the exponents.

$$x^{-7}x^3y^5z^4z^6=x^{-4}y^5z^{10}$$

$$x^{-7}y^5y^7z^4z^{-9}=x^{-7}y^{12}z^{-5}$$

steps.title: Combine the terms
steps.content: The product is $$x^{-4}y^5z^{10}+x^{-7}y^{12}z^{-5}$$ The answer is B.
